steps.title: Understand the key finding and its implication
steps.content: The passage establishes that EBF repels disease-carrying mosquitoes by activating Or31, a receptor shared across all such species. The question asks what researchers should do next to develop new repellents based on this mechanism.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong directions and confirm the correct one
steps.content:
• "identify molecular components similar to EBF that target the activation of Or31 receptors.": If Or31 is the key receptor and EBF works by activating it, finding other molecules that also activate Or31 is the direct path to new repellents. ✓
  • "investigate alternative methods for extracting EBF molecules from chrysanthemums.": Extraction methods address supply of EBF itself, not the development of new repellent compounds. This doesn't follow from the finding. ✗
  • "verify the precise locations of Or31 and other odor receptors on mosquitoes' antennae.": Anatomical mapping of receptor locations is not the logical next step after identifying which receptor EBF activates. ✗
  • "determine the maximum number of different odor receptors that can be activated by a single molecule.": This addresses receptor capacity in general, not the Or31-specific mechanism identified in the passage. ✗
That matches the correct answer — A is correct.
